message:
docs(readme): fix astype-to-Utf8 example — to-Utf8 cast unsupported (br-um5fl / fd90.174)

fd90.174: caught by fd90.173's integration test draft. README line 1011 had:

  let df2 = df.astype_columns(&[("id", DType::Utf8), ("score", DType::Float64)])?;

But cast_scalar_owned at crates/fp-types/src/lib.rs unconditionally
rejects DType::Utf8 as a cast target:

  DType::Utf8 => Err(TypeError::InvalidCast { from, to: target }),

So the example only works if 'id' is already Utf8 — not a useful
multi-cast demo. A user with an Int64 'id' column hits InvalidCast.

Pandas equivalent (df.astype('str')) works on any type via to_string;
FrankenPandas's design routes string conversion through Display
rather than the astype path, so DType::Utf8 isn't a valid cast target
today.

Fix: change the example to use Int64 + Float64 targets that work for
arbitrary numeric source columns. Inline note explains the to-Utf8
limitation and points readers to Display::to_string for that case.

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
